task_completion.description: Generate the baseline emissions XML report using HBEFA2 emission classes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a SUMO command generating emissions, a file manager showing 'baseline_emissions.xml' in '/home/ga/SUMO_Output/', or the XML file open in a text editor showing emission data.

task_completion.description: Implement a Low Emission Zone (LEZ) by restricting high-polluting vehicle types on at least five core edges using at least two distinct restriction strategies.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SUMO Netedit GUI showing edge restrictions (e.g., disallowed vehicle classes), or a text editor showing modifications to SUMO network (.net.xml) or route (.rou.xml) files adding restrictions like 'disallow="..."' for specific edges.

task_completion.description: Generate the LEZ emissions XML output after applying restrictions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a SUMO command running the modified LEZ scenario, or a file manager/editor showing 'lez_emissions.xml' in '/home/ga/SUMO_Output/'.

task_completion.description: Produce a comparative impact assessment CSV with specific columns and pollutants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'emission_impact_report.csv' in '/home/ga/SUMO_Output/' open in an editor or terminal. It must contain columns: 'pollutant', 'baseline_total_mg', 'lez_total_mg', and 'reduction_pct', with rows for CO2, CO, NOx, PMx, and HC.

task_completion.description: Document the LEZ design, numerical results, and policy recommendations in a professional summary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text file named 'emission_impact_summary.txt' in '/home/ga/SUMO_Output/' open in an editor, containing readable text that discusses the LEZ design, cites numerical results from the CSV, and provides policy recommendations.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually ran SUMO to generate the XML files rather than manually creating fake XMLs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal logs showing 'sumo' or 'sumo-gui' commands being executed with '--emission-output' flags, or a Python script using 'traci' or 'subprocess' to run SUMO.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV report was generated by processing the XML data, not by hallucinating numbers.
integrity.visual_evidence: A Python script, shell script, or spreadsheet application open on screen showing logic to parse the XML files and calculate the totals and reduction percentages.

integrity.description: Verify all files are saved in the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths in the terminal, file manager, or text editor explicitly show '/home/ga/SUMO_Output/' as the location for the baseline XML, LEZ XML, CSV, and TXT files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the 'bologna_pasubio' SUMO scenario. The agent must generate four specific files in /home/ga/SUMO_Output/: baseline_emissions.xml, lez_emissions.xml, emission_impact_report.csv, and emission_impact_summary.txt. The LEZ implementation requires restricting older/high-polluting vehicles on at least 5 edges using at least 2 distinct strategies (note: automatic verification may grant partial strategy credit if at least 1 edge is modified). The CSV must contain exactly the columns: pollutant, baseline_total_mg, lez_total_mg, and reduction_pct for the pollutants CO2, CO, NOx, PMx, and HC.
